potential_hallucinations.location: Paragraph 1: 'driving a 78% program adoption rate across 17 launches'
potential_hallucinations.detail: TWO ISSUES: (1) '17 launches' does NOT appear in content_library.md — only '15+' or '20+' programs. (2) '78% program adoption rate' is a third wording variant not in content_library.md. The library has 'client conversion rate' (L&D) and 'program enrollment rate' (PM/Engagement). 'Adoption rate' is neither. While all three are likely describing the same underlying metric, the specific term 'adoption rate' is not sourced.
potential_hallucinations.recommendation: Replace '78% program adoption rate across 17 launches' with '78% client conversion rate across 15+ program launches' (L&D variant, verified in content_library.md).

potential_hallucinations.claim: I also have hands-on experience with SAP, which I understand is Sandvik's system environment
potential_hallucinations.detail: content_library.md HRIS/Tools Statement says: 'I'm proficient in Greenhouse, Rippling, ADP, and SAP. I have extensive administrative experience in Greenhouse... and have used Rippling, ADP, and SAP at the user level for standard HRIS tasks.' So SAP experience EXISTS but is explicitly described as 'user level for standard HRIS tasks' — not hands-on implementation or admin-level. The cover letter says 'hands-on experience with SAP' which overstates the level relative to content_library.md.
potential_hallucinations.recommendation: Soften to: 'I have user-level experience with SAP from previous roles' or 'I'm familiar with SAP at the user level and would be comfortable in a Sandvik SAP environment.' Do not claim 'hands-on experience' when source material says 'user level for standard HRIS tasks'.

per_contact.name: Anthony Bowman
per_contact.search_query_used: Anthony Bowman Sandvik Gainesville Alachua HR linkedin
per_contact.notes: MISDESCRIPTION: targets.json describes Anthony Bowman as 'Former Sandvik — Gainesville area' but web search shows 'Anthony Bowman is... located in Gainesville... works at Sandvik' (present tense, active profile). The rationale in targets.json says he's 'now at The Arc' — however search results don't confirm that transition and show him still connected to Sandvik. Person is real and their Sandvik/Gainesville connection is confirmed. But describing as 'former' may be inaccurate — he may still be at Sandvik and could be contacted as a current employee. Either way the LinkedIn profile is real and the person is confirmed at or formerly at Sandvik Alachua. Treat as contactable.
per_contact.verdict: VERIFIED_BUT_MISDESCRIBED — may still be current Sandvik employee, not former
